eaip:Header.eaip:Title: Enterprise AI Agent Interoperability Protocol Technical Specification
eaip:Header.eaip:Abstract: This document defines the binding technical standards for all services participating in the Enterprise Agentic Integration Platform (EAIP). It mandates gRPC as the exclusive inter-service transport protocol, specifies the SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity architecture for zero-trust authentication, and formalizes the Recursive Context Envelope (RCE) protocol for distributed state propagation across agentic pipelines.

eaip:TransportLayer.eaip:Mandate: All synchronous request/response and streaming communication between EAIP services MUST be conducted over gRPC (HTTP/2 + Protocol Buffers). REST, GraphQL, and raw TCP are not permitted for inter-service calls within the mesh.
eaip:TransportLayer.eaip:Rationale: Binary Efficiency: Protocol Buffers serialization is 3–10x smaller and 20–100x faster to parse than equivalent JSON payloads.
Bidirectional Streaming: Native support for client-streaming, server-streaming, and full-duplex streams required by agentic pipeline patterns.
Schema-First Contract: Proto files serve as the canonical API contract.
mTLS Native: gRPC's HTTP/2 transport layer integrates seamlessly with SPIFFE mTLS.
eaip:TransportLayer.eaip:ChannelConfig: # Required configuration for all gRPC channels within EAIP mesh channel: transport: grpc tls: mode: mutual # MUST be mutual (mTLS) — one-way TLS not permitted cert_provider: spiffe # MUST use SPIFFE SVID — no static certs compression: gzip # SHOULD enable; MUST NOT use brotli max_recv_msg_size: 4194304 # 4 MiB max_send_msg_size: 4194304 keepalive: time: 30s timeout: 10s permit_without_stream: true retry_policy: max_attempts: 3 initial_backoff: 0.5s max_backoff: 10s backoff_multiplier: 2 retryable_status_codes: [UNAVAILABLE, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED]

eaip:IdentityLayer.eaip:ArchitectureOverview: The EAIP uses SPIFFE (Secure Production Identity Framework for Everyone) as the foundation for all workload identity. SPIRE (the SPIFFE Runtime Environment) serves as the identity control plane, issuing short-lived X.509 SVIDs to all workloads in the mesh.
eaip:IdentityLayer.eaip:TrustFederation: Cross-domain service calls require explicit trust bundle federation. Federation endpoints MUST be registered in both SPIRE servers using ClusterFederatedTrustDomain resources.
eaip:IdentityLayer.eaip:WorkloadAPIIntegration: Every EAIP service MUST integrate with the SPIRE Workload API (unix:///run/spire/sockets/agent.sock) to obtain credentials at runtime. Credentials baked into images are prohibited.

eaip:StateManagement.eaip:ProtocolOverview: The Recursive Context Envelope (RCE) is the canonical mechanism for propagating distributed state, trace context, causal lineage, and agentic session metadata across all EAIP service boundaries. Every gRPC message MUST carry an RCE as field 1.
eaip:StateManagement.eaip:DesignPrinciples: Recursive Nesting: Each hop appends its own context frame.
Immutable Lineage: trace_id and root_span_id are set once at ingress.
Mutable Session State: session_state map carries agentic variables.
eaip:StateManagement.eaip:PropagationRules: RCE-001: Receive inbound request: Validate checksum; reject if schema_version < 3. RCE-002: Begin processing: Append new ContextFrame with status=ACTIVE. RCE-003: Make downstream call: Forward envelope with all existing frames intact. RCE-005: Return response: Set own frame status=FINALIZED.

eaip:Appendix.eaip:Title: Migration Guide
eaip:Appendix.eaip:Content: Phased migration from REST+JWT to gRPC+SPIFFE: Phase 1: Deploy SPIRE Agents. Phase 2: Generate Proto definitions. Phase 3: Switch internal callers to gRPC. Phase 4: Enable SVID-only mTLS.

eaip:Appendix.eaip:Title: Identity Bootstrapping Diagram
eaip:Appendix.eaip:Content: sequenceDiagram autonumber participant WL as EAIP Agent participant SA as SPIRE Agent participant SS as SPIRE Server WL->>SA: FetchX509SVID() SA->>SS: Attest workload SS-->>SA: X.509 SVID SA-->>WL: SVID + Trust Bundle

eaip:Appendices.eaip:Changelog: 3.4.1 (2026-03-15): FINALIZED. Corrected RCE fan-in merge semantics. 3.0.0 (2025-09-01): Major revision: replaced JWT with SPIFFE/SPIRE.
